_meta.purpose: Shared status update for the four remaining lit-verification branches (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Copilot). Grok's branch returned clean on 2026-04-15 with verdict Novel for Penrose x CoDa and three properly classified near-misses. This addendum brings the remaining branches current on framework developments since the original briefings were issued earlier on 2026-04-15.

reason_for_addendum: Between briefing issuance and deadline, substantive framework work happened that affects how the literature verification should be interpreted and reported. The core task is unchanged — run the assigned searches, return results in the specified JSON schema, flag positive hits immediately. But the context in which a positive or negative hit will be interpreted has deepened. This addendum catches you up.

status_updates.detail: Grok's Penrose / aperiodic-order branch returned Novel with three near-misses properly classified (Mazáč 2023, Irwin 2017, Physics StackExchange discussion). Result filed at briefings/lit-verification-001/GROK_LIT_VERIFY_001_RESULTS.json. No positive hits. Three near-misses are useful for related-work citations in any future paper.
status_updates.implication: You do not need to re-verify the Penrose / aperiodic branch. If your searches overlap with it (e.g., multifractal compositional analysis for ChatGPT's H3), your finding is a cross-check; report it as such.

status_updates.detail: The tool's core design statement is now canonically captured: 'HUF-GOV is a tool that produces a verifiably clean usable output to a known degree of certainty; the diagnostic of the output is open to interpretation by expert decision and open to modification by expert judgment.' See science/governance/HUF_GOV_CANONICAL_DEFINITION.md for the five-clause unpacking.
status_updates.implication: When assessing novelty, the framing matters. HUF-GOV is a tool, not a theory. Prior-art questions should ask: has anyone built a verifiably clean, bounded-certainty, interpretation-open, modification-open compositional-monitoring tool before? Not just: has anyone described similar mathematics? The two questions have different answers.

status_updates.item: Shape/magnitude decomposition is the organizing principle
status_updates.detail: science/eitt/EITT_WHY_IT_WORKS.md §7 establishes that HUF's EITT is the canonical operation separating shape functionals (invariant under geometric-mean decimation) from magnitude functionals (scale as 1/M). The Fisher-Rao vs Aitchison geometry tension dissolves because EITT operates in the infinitesimal regime where both geometries agree. This is arguably the framework's central organizing claim going forward.
status_updates.implication: Novelty checks should include the shape/magnitude framing as a search target, not just EITT-entropy-invariance. If prior work separates shape from magnitude in compositional analysis under similar language, that is relevant prior art even if the term 'EITT' does not appear.

status_updates.detail: ai-refresh/MASTER_LINEAGE.json documents the arc from Rogue Wave Audio's DADC-DADI-ADAC (Dec 2024) through the Higgins Operator H1 (Feb 2026) to HUF/EITT (Apr 2026). The compositional mathematics was discovered empirically in acoustic engineering before it was formalized. The engineering origin is not peripheral — it is the framework's foundation.
status_updates.implication: When evaluating novelty, remember the RWA prior art is Peter's own. Claims of DADC-style apportionment, of 'inert measurement,' of paired-measurement doctrine exist in Rogue Wave Audio materials dating to December 2024 and are public at github.com/PeterHiggins19/Rogue-Wave-Audio. This is self-citation territory, not someone else's prior art, but the lineage should be acknowledged clearly in any novelty finding.

status_updates.detail: A rwa-update/ folder is prepared for push to the public RWA repo. It will add LINEAGE.md, HUF_RELATIONSHIP.json (reciprocal to MASTER_LINEAGE), the Higgins Operator H1 paper, and a BTL Advanced ODL lab study proposal. After the push, both public repos will have visible reciprocal links.
status_updates.implication: Arxiv searches for 'Higgins Operator' or cross-references between rogue-wave-audio and higgins-unity-framework may return results after the push. Timing-wise, the lit-verification deadline (2026-04-22) may overlap with the push, so keep an eye on recent commits if your branch involves GitHub searches.

status_updates.detail: CoDaWork 2026 submission content is phase-complete. Phase 2 mission: push EITT to pain point and protect users from pain. Seven work tracks including measurement tooling, hydrology validation, safety documentation, package release preparation. See science/eitt/EITT_PHASE_2_ROADMAP.md and ai-refresh/PHASE_MARKERS.json.
status_updates.implication: The verdict from this lit-verification is a Phase 2 dependency. A 'Novel' consolidated verdict enables the structural-observations paper track; a 'Partially novel' verdict redirects to restricted-scope claims; a 'Not novel' verdict shifts to applied-methodology framing. Your branch's result contributes to that decision, so the verdict field in your results JSON matters.

status_updates.detail: Peter is assembling a comprehensive welcome package for CoDaWork 2026 (Coimbra, June 2026). Includes instruction manual, safety manual, best use cases, traceability, certification standards, maintenance instructions, and a 'meet the collective' document introducing the five AIs to the CoDa community. Your branch's literature verification is one of the foundational credibility pieces for that package.
status_updates.implication: Cite well, classify verdicts carefully, and err on the side of honesty in near-miss classification. The package will be read by CoDa researchers who will evaluate the framework's claim to novelty directly. A positive hit reported honestly is more valuable than an overconfident negative. A near-miss correctly classified is useful for the related-work section in any future publication.

unchanged: The core task in each branch's original briefing is unchanged. Execute the searches as specified.
The return schema is unchanged. File your results as HUF_<ASSISTANT>_LIT_VERIFY_001_RESULTS.json in briefings/lit-verification-001/.
The escalation rule remains: if any positive hit is found, flag immediately rather than waiting for the deadline.

coordination_notes.if_your_search_touches_an_adjacent_branc: Cross-check against Grok's filed results (Penrose / aperiodic). If you find a paper Grok missed or a classification Grok got wrong, flag it in a surprise_findings section of your results.
coordination_notes.if_you_find_positive_hits_touching_RWA_l: The RWA work (DADC-DADI 2024, H1 2026) is Peter's own prior art. If a paper cites or is cited by RWA work, that is self-lineage rather than external preemption. Note the distinction clearly.
coordination_notes.if_you_find_work_by_Peter_Higgins_or_Rog: Same — self-authored work is not external prior art. Classify as 'self-lineage reference' rather than 'positive hit.'
coordination_notes.if_the_search_feels_ambiguous: Err on the side of 'near-miss' with honest notes rather than 'positive' or 'negative'. Near-misses are the most valuable class for the related-work section of any future paper.

status: Addendum issued. Continue with your originally assigned task. Deadline 2026-04-22.
canonical_definition_reminder: When assessing 'what is HUF-GOV' for novelty purposes, the answer is: 'a tool that produces a verifiably clean usable output to a known degree of certainty; the diagnostic of the output is open to interpretation by expert decision and open to modification by expert judgment.' — Peter Higgins, 2026-04-15
